ch5_start.text: A new cycle. Security Hub is half-dark. David is not the only problem on the board — but he may be the one who blinded everyone else.

ch5_hub.text: Where do you dig before the next scream writes your schedule for you?

choices.text: End chapter — follow the next emergency

ch5_security.text: David sits among blank monitors, rubbing his chest as if breathing costs interest.
ch5_security.speaker: David

choices.text: Confront him about the wiped cameras.
Ask what he needs to keep the station safe.

ch5_quarters.text: A worn novel hides a folded Earth-side diagnosis. Stage 4. Six months. Get your affairs in order.

choices.text: Put it back. Return to hub.

ch5_armory.text: The armory still opens. Behind the loose grate: Abyssal Quartz packed for smuggling — condensate beading on the seals.

ch5_armory_sealed.text: Biometric red. David meant what he said. When the organism comes, these weapons will not be yours.

ch5_server_closet.text: Heat-dead racks. One drive still ticks. You pull a three-second fragment from before the wipe: a small silhouette in a vent — then static.

ch5_sector_b.text: Sector B's lower seal weeps condensation. Sensors chirp flood warnings that Miller once silenced. Soon this place will be a grave with a corridor number.

choices.text: Mark the failure points and leave.

ch5_soft.text: 'I need people to stop asking questions I can't answer,' David says. His eyes flick to a dead camera bank. 'And I need time.'
ch5_soft.speaker: David

choices.text: Press him with the Server Wipe Audit.

ch5_confront_menu.text: David's jaw tightens. 'Say what you came to say.'
ch5_confront_menu.speaker: David

choices.text: Accuse him of sabotaging the station and covering Miller's death.
Present the wipe audit with his smuggling stash and diagnosis.
Present only the wipe audit — force a confession.

ch5_accuse_wrong.text: 'Infected? Working for that thing?' David seals the armory. 'Find your own guns when it comes.'
ch5_accuse_wrong.speaker: David

ch5_accuse_partial.text: With only the wipe log, David goes cold. 'That proves I deleted files. Not murder. That's all you get.'
ch5_accuse_partial.speaker: David

ch5_accuse_correct.text: You lay out the wipe, the quartz, the diagnosis. David folds. 'I scrubbed the feeds so Corporate wouldn't see the smuggling. I didn't know something else came through the breach.'
ch5_accuse_correct.speaker: David

choices.text: Offer secrecy for future overrides — then return to hub.
Condemn him, then return to hub.

ch5_end.text: A muffled scream rolls through the hull — from the Comms Tower. The optional doors are closing. The mandatory ones are opening.

textVariants.text: A muffled scream rolls through the hull. For a second it sounds like your own voice, pitched wrong. Comms Tower. Now.
